message.content:
You are a senior code reviewer. Review the following changes for task L03570 "Wire up demo data and compose full UI" in a Raycast UI clone project.

The task requirements:
- Create mock data (fake commands, apps, bookmarks) in src/data/mock_data.ts
- Define TypeScript interfaces for ListItemData, SectionData in src/types.ts
- Render SearchBar + List with sections + ActionPanel together in App.tsx
- Filter list items based on search input (case-insensitive substring match)
- Demonstrate keyboard navigation working end-to-end
- Show Detail panel for selected item

Project conventions:
- TypeScript strict mode
- React 19
- Sass (no CSS modules, no styled-components, no tailwind)
- No comments in code unless JSDoc needed
- snake_case file naming
- No 'as any' or workarounds
- No over-engineering

Please read these files and provide a thorough review:
1. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/types.ts
2. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/data/mock_data.ts
3. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx
4. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/command_palette/command_palette.scss
5. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/command_palette/command_palette.tsx
6. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/list/list.tsx
7. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/detail/detail.tsx
8. /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/detail/detail_metadata.tsx

Check for:
- Type safety issues (no 'as any', proper generics)
- Correct composition of all components
- Search filtering correctness
- Keyboard navigation integration
- Detail panel display for selected item
- Layout correctness (split view: list on left ~40%, detail on right ~60%)
- Missing edge cases
- Unused imports
- Any bugs or issues

Return a structured review with: APPROVED items, ISSUES (must fix), and SUGGESTIONS (nice to have).

content.text: I'll read all the specified files to conduct a thorough review.

content.text: Now let me check if the `ListContext` is using React 19's `createContext` API correctly (the `value` prop on provider).

content.content:
1:import { createContext, useContext } from 'react';
8:export const ListContext = createContext<ListContextValue | null>(null);

content.text:
Good. React 19 supports using `<Context value={...}>` directly instead of `<Context.Provider value={...}>`, which is what `list.tsx` line 96 does. That checks out.

Now let me verify the layout split percentages and one more thing about query reset behavior.

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the structured review.

---

# Code Review: Task L03570 - Wire Up Demo Data and Compose Full UI

## APPROVED

**1. Type definitions (`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/types.ts`)**
- Clean discriminated union for `ListItemMetadataEntry` using `type` field. Exhaustive switch in `renderMetadataEntry` naturally covers all cases.
- No `as any`, no loose types. `TagColor` import from the component is appropriate since it defines the source of truth.
- `SectionData` and `ListItemData` interfaces are minimal and sufficient.

**2. Mock data (`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/data/mock_data.ts`)**
- Good variety: Suggestions, Commands, Applications -- covers the "fake commands, apps, bookmarks" requirement.
- Every item has a `detail` with `markdown` and `metadata`, exercising all metadata entry types (label, link, tag-list, separator).
- All IDs are unique. All data conforms to `SectionData[]` without casts.

**3. Search filtering logic (App.tsx lines 13-26)**
- Case-insensitive substring match on `title` and `subtitle` -- correct per requirements.
- Empty sections are pruned via `.filter(section => section.items.length > 0)`.
- Empty query short-circuits to full data -- good.

**4. Component composition (App.tsx)**
- Clean hierarchy: `CommandPalette > SearchBar + body(List + Detail) + ActionPanel`.
- `renderMetadataEntry` correctly maps all discriminated union variants with exhaustive switch.
- The IIFE pattern for `globalIndex` tracking (lines 121-140) is functional and avoids stale closure issues since `globalIndex` is scoped per render.

**5. Keyboard navigation (list.tsx)**
- Arrow up/down with wrapping. Enter dispatches `onAction`. Modifier keys are excluded.
- `scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' })` keeps active item visible.
- `onActiveIndexChange` callback propagates index to parent for detail panel display.
- Mouse hover also updates active index.

**6. Detail panel rendering**
- Markdown sanitized with DOMPurify, rendered via `marked`. `{ async: false }` ensures synchronous return -- correct usage.
- Metadata sub-components (Label, Link, TagList, Tag, Separator) are composed via `DetailMetadata` compound component pattern with `Object.assign`.

**7. SCSS and layout**
- `.command-palette__body` is `display: flex` with `min-height: 0` and `overflow: hidden` -- correct flex container for split view.
- `.command-palette__list-container` is `flex: 1` (takes available space). `.detail` is also `flex: 1` with `border-left`. This creates an even 50/50 split when both are present.
- SCSS uses Sass tokens consistently, no CSS modules or tailwind.

**8. Accessibility**
- `role="listbox"` on list, `role="option"` + `aria-selected` on items, `role="search"` on search bar, `role="region"` + `aria-label` on detail, `role="group"` + `aria-labelledby` on sections. Solid.

---

## ISSUES (Must Fix)

### ISSUE 1: `selectedIndex` is NOT reset when query changes, causing stale/out-of-bounds selection

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`

When the user types a query, `filtered` changes, so `allItems` shrinks. But `selectedIndex` in App state stays at its old value. The `List` component internally clamps its own `activeIndex` via the `useEffect` on line 36-46 of `list.tsx`, but `App.selectedIndex` is only updated via the `onActiveIndexChange` callback. The clamping effect calls `setActiveIndex` which fires `onActiveIndexChange`, so the parent does eventually get updated -- but there is a **one render frame** where `selectedIndex` in App is stale and `allItems[selectedIndex]` could be `undefined`, or worse, the **wrong item**.

Consider this scenario:
1. User has 12 items, selects index 8 (e.g., "Visual Studio Code").
2. User types "calc" -- filtered list has 1 item, index 0.
3. On the first render after the query change: `allItems` has 1 item, `selectedIndex` is still 8. `selectedItem` is `undefined`, detail panel disappears for a frame.
4. The `List` useEffect clamps to 0, fires `onActiveIndexChange(0)`, and on the next render it corrects.

This is a visual flicker bug. The detail panel briefly disappears and reappears.

**Fix:** Reset `selectedIndex` to 0 whenever `query` changes:

```tsx
const handleQueryChange = useCallback((value: string) => {
  setQuery(value);
  setSelectedIndex(0);
}, []);

// And pass handleQueryChange instead of setQuery to SearchBar:
<SearchBar value={query} onChange={handleQueryChange} />
```

### ISSUE 2: Layout split is 50/50, not ~40% list / ~60% detail as required

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/command_palette/command_palette.scss` and `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/detail/detail.scss`

Both `.command-palette__list-container` and `.detail` use `flex: 1`, which produces a 50/50 split. The task specifies "list on left ~40%, detail on right ~60%."

**Fix:** In `command_palette.scss`, change the list container:

```scss
.command-palette__list-container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  flex: 0 0 40%;
  min-width: 0;
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

Or alternatively, set `flex: 2` on `.detail` and keep `flex: 1` on the list container (which yields a 33/66 split -- too far). The cleanest approach is explicit percentages: `flex: 0 0 40%` on the list, and keep `flex: 1` on detail.

### ISSUE 3: `useCallback` with empty dependency array for `handleActiveIndexChange` is unnecessary abstraction

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`, line 99-101

```tsx
const handleActiveIndexChange = useCallback((index: number) => {
  setSelectedIndex(index);
}, []);
```

This is not a bug, but `setSelectedIndex` is already a stable reference (React guarantees state setters are stable). Wrapping it in `useCallback` with `[]` is functionally identical to just passing `setSelectedIndex` directly. However, this becomes a **real issue** once you apply the fix from ISSUE 1 -- because the handler will need to do more than just `setSelectedIndex`, or the query-change reset will need its own path. After ISSUE 1 fix, make sure this stays coherent.

---

## SUGGESTIONS (Nice to Have)

### SUGGESTION 1: Use stable IDs instead of array index for `key` in `renderMetadataEntry`

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`, line 32

```tsx
function renderMetadataEntry(entry: ListItemMetadataEntry, index: number) {
```

Using `index` as `key` is acceptable here because metadata entries are static and don't reorder. But if metadata becomes dynamic in the future, consider generating keys from `entry.type + entry.title` (for non-separator entries) or `separator-${index}`.

### SUGGESTION 2: Consider adding a `key` based on section title + item ID for IIFE mapping

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`, line 124

`<ListSection key={section.title}>` -- this works only if section titles are unique, which they are in the current mock data. If sections could share titles in the future, this would cause key collisions. Low risk now but worth noting.

### SUGGESTION 3: Mock data lacks icon data

The task mentions "fake commands, apps, bookmarks" and the `ListItemData` type supports `icon?: ReactNode`. None of the mock items provide icons. In a Raycast clone, every list item has an icon. This is a visual fidelity gap. Consider adding simple SVG or emoji icons to the mock data for a more realistic demo.

### SUGGESTION 4: The `ListItem` accessory uses `key={i}` (array index)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/list/list_item/list_item.tsx`, line 54

This is fine for static accessory lists but flagging for awareness.

### SUGGESTION 5: No `onAction` handler is wired in App

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`

The `List` component accepts `onAction` (fires on Enter key), but `App` does not pass it. Enter keypress currently does nothing visible. The task says "demonstrate keyboard navigation working end-to-end" -- navigation is there (arrow keys + selection highlight + detail panel update), but Enter is a dead end. Consider at minimum a no-op handler or a console log to show it works.

### SUGGESTION 6: `detail.scss` internal metadata split is reversed from the outer split

The outer split is list ~40% / detail ~60% (once ISSUE 2 is fixed). Inside the detail panel with metadata, `.detail__content` gets 60% and `.detail__metadata` gets 40%. This is fine design-wise (more room for markdown), but worth confirming this matches the Raycast reference.

---

## Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Approved | 8 items |
| Issues (must fix) | 3 |
| Suggestions | 6 |

The architecture is clean, type-safe, and well-composed. The two critical issues are: (1) the stale selection index on query change causing a visual flicker where the detail panel disappears for a frame, and (2) the layout split not matching the 40/60 requirement. Issue 3 is minor but becomes relevant once issue 1 is fixed. Everything else is solid work.
